auth.usage_instructions: Manage Slack API authentication. Login, logout, check status, refresh tokens, and test credentials. Supports multiple profiles via --profile/-p.

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.
login.usage_instructions: Authenticate with Slack. Uses OAuth flow (opens browser). Use --profile/-p to save to a named profile. Use --force/-F to re-authenticate. Use --credential-type/-c to authenticate only a specific credential type.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

logout.help: Clear stored credentials and browser sessions.
logout.usage_instructions: Clear stored Slack credentials. Use --profile/-p to clear a specific profile.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

profiles.usage_instructions: Manage authentication profiles for multiple Slack workspaces. Create, list, switch default, and delete profiles.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Create a new profile from .env.example template. NAME is required (positional, e.g., staging, production).

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Delete a profile and its data by NAME (required). Use --force/-F to skip confirmation.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.
get.usage_instructions: Get details for a specific profile by NAME (required, positional). Use --table/-t.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.
list.usage_instructions: List all profiles and show which is the default. Use --filter/-f (e.g., is_default:eq:True), --limit/-l, --properties/-p, --table/-t.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

remove.help: Delete a profile and its data.

select.help: Activate a profile within its auth type.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to activate within its auth type

status.usage_instructions: Check current authentication status. Use --profile/-p to check a specific profile. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Verify credentials work by testing against Slack API. Use --verbose/-v for detailed checks. Use --profile/-p for a specific profile.

cache.usage_instructions: Manage the CLI response cache. Use `cache clear` to remove all cached responses.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Remove all cached API responses. Frees disk space and ensures fresh data on next commands.

canvas.usage_instructions: Manage Slack canvases (collaborative documents). List canvases in the workspace or a specific channel, and retrieve canvas content.

get.help: Get canvas content. Example: slack canvas get F1234567890 slack canvas get F1234567890 --table slack canvas get F1234567890 --html
get.usage_instructions: Get canvas content by CANVAS_ID (required, e.g. F1234567890). Use --html to get raw HTML instead of text. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

options.name: --html
options.help: Output raw HTML instead of text

list.help: List canvases in the workspace or a specific channel. Example: slack canvas list --table slack canvas list C1234567890 --table slack canvas list --limit 50
list.usage_instructions: List canvases in the workspace or a specific channel. CHANNEL_ID is optional — omit for all canvases. Use --limit/-l (default 100), --filter/-f, --properties/-p. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

arguments.name: channel_id
arguments.help: Channel ID to list canvases for (optional, lists all if not specified)

channels.usage_instructions: Manage Slack channels: list, get info, create, archive, join, leave, and list members. Channel IDs look like C1234567890.

archive.help: Archive a channel. Example: slack channels archive C1234567890
archive.usage_instructions: Archive a channel by CHANNEL_ID (required, positional).

create.help: Create a new channel. Example: slack channels create my-new-channel slack channels create my-private-channel --private
create.usage_instructions: Create a new channel. NAME is required (positional). Use --private to create a private channel.

get.help: Get information about a specific channel. Example: slack channels get C1234567890 slack channels get C1234567890 --table
get.usage_instructions: Get info about a specific channel by CHANNEL_ID (required). Use --table/-t for formatted output.

join.help: Join a channel. Example: slack channels join C1234567890
join.usage_instructions: Join a channel by CHANNEL_ID (required, positional).

leave.help: Leave a channel. Example: slack channels leave C1234567890
leave.usage_instructions: Leave a channel by CHANNEL_ID (required, positional).

list.help: List channels in the workspace. Example: slack channels list --table slack channels list --types public_channel --limit 50 slack channels list --filter "is_private:eq:true" slack channels list --filter "name:ilike:%general%" slack channels list --unread --table
list.usage_instructions: List channels in the workspace. Use --types to filter (public_channel, private_channel, mpim, im; default: public_channel,private_channel). Use --unread/-u for channels with unread messages only. Use --include-archived for archived channels. Use --filter/-f (e.g., name:ilike:%general%), --limit/-l, --properties/-p.

options.name: --unread
options.help: Only show channels with unread messages

members.help: List members of a channel. Returns a list of user IDs by default. Use --resolve to get full user info. Example: slack channels members C1234567890 slack channels members C1234567890 --table slack channels members C1234567890 --resolve --table slack channels members C1234567890 --resolve --filter "is_bot:eq:false"
members.usage_instructions: List members of a channel by CHANNEL_ID (required). Returns user IDs by default; use --resolve/-r to get full user info. Use --filter/-f (only with --resolve, e.g., is_bot:eq:false). Use --limit/-l, --table/-t.

options.name: --resolve
options.help: Resolve user IDs to user info

options.name: --filter
options.help: Filter results (field:op:value). Only applies when --resolve is used

dm.usage_instructions: Manage direct messages: list DM conversations, get DM info, send DMs, and read DM history. Users can be specified by user ID, email, or @username.

get.help: Get a direct message conversation with a user. Example: slack dm get U1234567890 slack dm get user@example.com --table
get.usage_instructions: Get DM conversation info for a USER (required — user ID, email, or @username). Use --table/-t for formatted output.

list.help: List direct message conversations. Example: slack dm list --table slack dm list --limit 20 slack dm list --unread --table slack dm list --include-group --table
list.usage_instructions: List DM conversations. Use --unread/-u for DMs with unread messages only. Use --filter/-f, --limit/-l, --properties/-p, --table/-t.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of DMs to list

options.name: --unread
options.help: Only show DMs with unread messages

read.help: Read direct message history with a user or group DM. For 1:1 DMs, specify the user: - User ID (U1234567890) - Email address (user@example.com) - Username (@john.doe or john.doe) For group DMs, specify the channel ID directly: - Channel ID (C01U109E658) - Find channel IDs with: slack dm list --include-group --table Example: slack dm read U1234567890 --table slack dm read user@example.com --limit 20 slack dm read @john.doe --table slack dm read C01U109E658 --table  # group DM by channel ID
read.usage_instructions: Read DM history with a USER (required — user ID, email, or @username). Use --limit/-l (default 50), --oldest/--latest for time range, --filter/-f for client-side filtering, --table/-t.

arguments.name: user
arguments.help: User ID, email, @username, or channel ID (for group DMs)

options.name: --filter
options.help: Client-side filter (field:op:value). Operators: eq, ne, gt, gte, lt, lte, in, nin, like, ilike, null, notnull

send.help: Send a direct message to a user or group DM. For 1:1 DMs, specify the user: - User ID (U1234567890) - Email address (user@example.com) - Username (@john.doe or john.doe) For group DMs, specify the channel ID directly: - Channel ID (C01U109E658) - Find channel IDs with: slack dm list --include-group --table Example: slack dm send U1234567890 "Hello!" slack dm send user@example.com "Hello via email lookup!" slack dm send @john.doe "Hello John!" slack dm send C01U109E658 "Hello group!"  # group DM by channel ID
send.usage_instructions: Send a DM to a user. USER (user ID, email, or @username) and TEXT are both required positional args. Use --thread-ts to reply in a thread. Remember: Slack uses mrkdwn, not standard markdown.

arguments.name: user
arguments.help: User ID, email, @username, or channel ID (for group DMs)

files.usage_instructions: Manage Slack files: list, upload, get info, download, and delete files in the workspace.

delete.help: Delete a file. Example: slack files delete F1234567890
delete.usage_instructions: Delete a file by FILE_ID (required, positional).

download.help: Download a file from Slack. Example: slack files download F1234567890 slack files download F1234567890 --output ./downloads/report.pdf slack files download F1234567890 -o ~/Documents/
download.usage_instructions: Download a file by FILE_ID (required). Use --output/-o to specify output path (defaults to current directory with original filename).

options.name: --output
options.help: Output path (defaults to current directory with original filename)

get.help: Get information about a file. Example: slack files get F1234567890 slack files get F1234567890 --table
get.usage_instructions: Get info about a file by FILE_ID (required, e.g. F1234567890). Use --table/-t for formatted output.

list.help: List files in the workspace. API filters (server-side): --channel, --user, --types, --ts-from, --ts-to Client-side filters: --filter for name, size, filetype, etc. Example: slack files list --table slack files list --channel C1234567890 slack files list --user U1234567890 --count 50 slack files list --types images slack files list --filter "size:gt:1000000" slack files list --filter "filetype:eq:pdf"
list.usage_instructions: List files in the workspace. API filters (server-side): --channel/-c, --user/-u, --types (images, pdfs, etc.), --ts-from, --ts-to. Client-side: --filter/-f (e.g., filetype:eq:pdf, size:gt:1000000). Use --limit/-l, --count, --page, --properties/-p, --table/-t.

options.name: --types
options.help: Filter by file type: all, spaces, snippets, images, gdocs, zips, pdfs (API filter)

options.name: --ts-from
options.help: Filter files created after this timestamp (API filter)

options.name: --ts-to
options.help: Filter files created before this timestamp (API filter)

upload.help: Upload a file to Slack. Example: slack files upload document.pdf --channels C1234567890 slack files upload image.png --channels C1234567890,C9876543210 --title "Screenshot"
upload.usage_instructions: Upload a file. FILE_PATH is required (positional). Use --channels/-c for comma-separated channel IDs to share to. Use --title/-t for file title, --comment for initial comment.

messages.usage_instructions: Send, read, search, and manage Slack messages. Use @Name in message text to auto-mention users. Channel IDs (C1234567890) and timestamps identify messages.

delete.help: Delete a message. Example: slack messages delete C1234567890 1234567890.123456
delete.usage_instructions: Delete a message by CHANNEL and TS (timestamp). Both are required positional args.

get.help: Get a specific message by channel and timestamp. Example: slack messages get C1234567890 1234567890.123456 slack messages get C1234567890 1234567890.123456 --table
get.usage_instructions: Get a specific message by CHANNEL and TS (timestamp). Both are required positional args. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

list.help: List messages from a channel. API filters (server-side): --oldest, --latest (timestamp-based) Client-side filters: --filter for user, text, type, etc. Example: slack messages list C1234567890 --table slack messages list C1234567890 --limit 50 slack messages list C1234567890 --filter "user:eq:U1234567890" slack messages list C1234567890 --filter "type:eq:message"
list.usage_instructions: List messages from a channel. CHANNEL is required (positional). API filters: --oldest, --latest (timestamps). Client-side: --filter/-f (e.g., user:eq:U1234567890). Use --limit/-l (default 100), --properties/-p, --table/-t.

mentions.help: List messages that mention you. Searches for messages where you are mentioned (@you) across all channels. Example: slack messages mentions --table slack messages mentions --count 50
mentions.usage_instructions: List messages that mention you (@you) across all channels. Use --count/-c (default 20), --filter/-f, --table/-t.

options.name: --filter
options.help: Client-side filter (field:op:value). Operators: eq, ne, gt, gte, lt, lte, in, nin, like, ilike, null, notnull

search.help: Search messages in the workspace. API filter: query argument (Slack search syntax) Client-side filters: --filter for additional filtering on results Example: slack messages search "hello world" slack messages search "error" --table slack messages search "error" --count 50 slack messages search "from:@user" --filter "channel.name:eq:general"
search.usage_instructions: Search messages across the workspace. QUERY is required (uses Slack search syntax, e.g., 'from:@user in:#channel'). Use --count/-c (default 20), --page/-p, --filter/-f for client-side filtering, --table/-t.

options.name: --filter
options.help: Client-side filter (field:op:value). Operators: eq, ne, gt, gte, lt, lte, in, nin, like, ilike, null, notnull

send.help: Send a message to a channel. Mentions: Use @Name or @First Last to mention users. The CLI will automatically convert these to proper Slack mentions. Example: slack messages send C1234567890 "Hello, World!" slack messages send C1234567890 "Hello @John Smith" slack messages send C1234567890 "Reply" --thread-ts 1234567890.123456
send.usage_instructions: Send a message to a channel. CHANNEL (ID or name) and TEXT are required positional args. Use --thread-ts to reply in a thread. Use --username for bot username override. Use @Name or @First Last in text for auto-mention. Remember: Slack uses mrkdwn formatting.

threads.help: List replies in a thread. API filters (server-side): --oldest, --latest (timestamp-based) Client-side filters: --filter for user, text, type, etc. Example: slack messages threads C1234567890 1234567890.123456 --table slack messages threads C1234567890 1234567890.123456 --limit 50 slack messages threads C1234567890 1234567890.123456 --filter "user:eq:U1234567890"
threads.usage_instructions: List replies in a thread. CHANNEL and THREAD_TS (parent message timestamp) are required positional args. API filters: --oldest, --latest. Client-side: --filter/-f. Use --limit/-l (default 100), --table/-t.

arguments.name: thread_ts
arguments.help: Timestamp of the parent message (thread root)

options.name: --filter
options.help: Client-side filter (field:op:value). Operators: eq, ne, gt, gte, lt, lte, in, nin, like, ilike, null, notnull

notifications.usage_instructions: View Slack notifications: unread DMs, channel mentions, thread replies, and saved items. Uses fast client.counts API when available (xoxc tokens).

counts.help: Show notification counts using Slack's internal API (fast). This uses the client.counts API which gives accurate badge counts for: - Unread DMs and group DMs - Channel mentions (@you) - Unread thread replies Requires xoxc (session) token. Falls back to slower method for other token types. Example: slack notifications counts --table
counts.usage_instructions: Show notification badge counts (unread DMs, channel mentions, thread replies). Uses fast client.counts API. Requires xoxc session token. Use --table/-t.

get.help: Get notification details for a specific channel. Example: slack notifications get C1234567890 slack notifications get C1234567890 --table
get.usage_instructions: Get notification details for a specific CHANNEL_ID (required). Use --table/-t for formatted output.

arguments.name: channel_id
arguments.help: Channel ID to get notification info for

list.help: List all notifications that contribute to your Slack badge. Uses the fast client.counts API when available (xoxc tokens). Shows: - Unread DMs and group DMs - Channel mentions (@you) - Unread thread replies - Saved for later items Example: slack notifications list --table slack notifications list --no-threads --table
list.usage_instructions: List all notifications contributing to your Slack badge. Use --no-dms, --no-threads, --no-mentions, --no-saved to exclude categories. Use --limit/-l, --filter/-f, --properties/-p, --table/-t.

summary.help: Show a quick summary of notification counts. Uses the fast client.counts API when available. Shows the actual badge counts that Slack uses for the app icon badge. Example: slack notifications summary
summary.usage_instructions: Quick summary of notification counts (badge counts). Uses fast client.counts API when available.

reminders.usage_instructions: Manage Slack reminders and saved items (Later). List, get, create, complete, and delete reminders across profiles.

complete.help: Mark a reminder as complete. Example: slack reminders complete Rm12345678 slack reminders complete Rm12345678 --table
complete.usage_instructions: Mark a reminder as complete by REMINDER_ID (required). Use --table/-t.

arguments.name: reminder_id
arguments.help: The ID of the reminder to complete

delete.help: Delete a reminder. Example: slack reminders delete Rm12345678 slack reminders delete Rm12345678 --table
delete.usage_instructions: Delete a reminder by REMINDER_ID (required). Use --table/-t.

arguments.name: reminder_id
arguments.help: The ID of the reminder to delete

get.help: Get a specific reminder by ID. Example: slack reminders get Rm12345678 slack reminders get Rm12345678 --table
get.usage_instructions: Get a specific reminder by REMINDER_ID (required, e.g. Rm12345678). Use --table/-t.

list.help: List all reminders from both traditional reminders and saved items (Later). Queries both reminders.list and saved.list APIs, normalizes results into a unified list. Example: slack reminders list slack reminders list --state all slack reminders list --all-profiles slack reminders list --profile devolutions slack reminders list --state all --table slack reminders list --content --table
list.usage_instructions: List all reminders from both traditional reminders and saved items (Later). Use --state/-s (in_progress, archived, completed, all; default: in_progress). Use --content/-c to fetch message content (slower). Use --profile for a specific profile, --all-profiles/-a for all profiles. Use --filter/-f, --limit/-l, --properties/-p, --table/-t.

options.name: --state
options.help: Filter by state: in_progress, archived, completed, all

options.name: --content
options.help: Fetch and include message content for saved items (slower)

options.name: --profile
options.help: Query a specific profile instead of the default

new.help: Save a message for later with an optional reminder time. Uses Slack's 'Later' feature (saved.add API) to save messages. Example: slack reminders new C01234567 1734625200.123456 slack reminders new C01234567 1734625200.123456 --due "30 min" slack reminders new C01234567 1734625200.123456 --due "2 hours" slack reminders new C01234567 1734625200.123456 --due "3 days" slack reminders new C01234567 1734625200.123456 --table
new.usage_instructions: Save a message for later with optional reminder time. CHANNEL and TS are required positional args. Use --due/-d for reminder time (e.g., '30 min', '2 hours', '3 days', '1 week'). Use --table/-t.

arguments.name: channel
arguments.help: The channel ID containing the message

options.name: --due
options.help: When to be reminded: e.g., '30 min', '2 hours', '3 days', '1 week'

users.usage_instructions: Manage Slack workspace users: list users, get user info, and set your status.

get.help: Get information about a specific user. Example: slack users get U1234567890 slack users get U1234567890 --table
get.usage_instructions: Get info about a specific user by USER_ID (required, e.g. U1234567890). Use --table/-t.

list.help: List users in the workspace. Deleted users are hidden by default. Use --include-deleted to show them. Example: slack users list --table slack users list --include-deleted --table slack users list --limit 50 slack users list --filter "is_admin:eq:true"
list.usage_instructions: List workspace users. Deleted users are hidden by default; use --include-deleted to show them. Use --filter/-f (e.g., is_admin:eq:true, name:eq:john), --limit/-l (default 100), --properties/-p, --table/-t.

set-photo.help: Set your profile photo. Example: slack users set-photo ./avatar.png
set-photo.usage_instructions: Set the authenticated Slack user profile photo from IMAGE_PATH. Use this when changing your own avatar; combine with top-level --profile to target a specific workspace/profile.

arguments.name: image_path
arguments.help: Path to the profile image file

set-status.help: Set your status. Example: slack users set-status "In a meeting" --emoji 📆 slack users set-status "Away" --emoji 🌴 --expiration 1640000000
set-status.usage_instructions: Set your Slack status. TEXT is required (positional). Use --emoji/-e for status emoji. Use --expiration for Unix timestamp when status expires.

usage_instructions: Use for all Slack workspace operations: sending/reading messages, managing channels, DMs, files, notifications, users, canvases, and reminders. Commands follow `slack <group> <action>` pattern. Most list commands support --table/-t, --filter/-f, --limit/-l, and --properties/-p. Use --no-cache to bypass response cache. Slack uses mrkdwn formatting (bold = *text*, links = <url|text>).
